Definition / What to try: Rewrite spoken sentences into shortest meaning-preserving phrases.
Mechanism: Reduces verbal load while preserving gist.
Expected Direction: Better gist/action recall than full transcript overlays.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Full transcript vs semantic compression on same clip.
Primary Metrics: Gist recall; action recall; perceived effort

Definition / What to try: Show the underlying claim rather than the literal words.
Mechanism: Lets viewer process mental model instead of syntax.
Failure Mode: May distort nuance if proposition is wrong.

Definition / What to try: Begin overlay with logical operator: Not, Stop, Less, First, Then.
Mechanism: Reveals relation before content; supports phrase structure.
Expected Direction: Better contrast recall than keyword-only captions.

Definition / What to try: Use two-line contrasts: Not X / Y, Stop X / Start Y.
Mechanism: Uses binary structure to reduce interpretation cost.
Expected Direction: Strong for myth-busting and advice content.

Definition / What to try: Display three labels across the clip: Problem, Cause, Fix.
Mechanism: Externalizes structure and reduces working-memory demand.
Expected Direction: Better action recall for educational clips.
Failure Mode: Can feel like a slide deck if overused.

Definition / What to try: Show a rule, a good example, then a wrong example.
Failure Mode: Too slow for very short clips.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Learning clip with rule-only vs rule-example-nonexample.

Definition / What to try: Label claim, proof, and takeaway as separate beats.
Mechanism: Helps viewers trust and encode the argument.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Advice clip with claim/payoff labels vs no labels.

Definition / What to try: Show the final point in the first second, then explain.
Mechanism: Reduces uncertainty and raises reward prediction.

Definition / What to try: For advice clips, show only the next action, not explanation.
Failure Mode: Weak context if viewer misses why.

Definition / What to try: End with a short, repeatable phrase: Systems beat mood.
Mechanism: Creates a verbal chunk for later recall.

Definition / What to try: Preserve verbs over nouns when showing fewer words.
Mechanism: Actions drive what to do next.

Definition / What to try: Display one small concept per beat that accumulates meaning.
Move from concrete to abstract: shoes → start → habit.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Beginner viewers: ladder vs direct concept.

Definition / What to try: Test 100%, 60%, 30%, 15%, and 5% transcript equivalents.
Expected Direction: Identifies optimal text density by content type.
Failure Mode: Too low loses context; too high overloads.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Same clip at multiple compression ratios.

Definition / What to try: Convert isolated keywords into 2–4 word phrase chunks.
Failure Mode: Slightly more text may hurt visual recall.

Reference Label: SEM-016 / OIOS — One Idea, One Screen Rule
Definition / What to try: Never show two unrelated ideas in one overlay event.
Expected Direction: Lower effort and better sequence recall.
Failure Mode: Too many rapid swaps if script is dense.

Reference Label: SEM-017 / GET — Gist vs Exact Mode Toggle
Definition / What to try: Use gist overlay for retention, closed captions for exact speech.
Expected Direction: Better overall satisfaction with accessibility preserved.
Failure Mode: Requires platform support or extra production effort.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Full captions plus gist overlay vs overlay alone.

Definition / What to try: Remove basically, actually, kind of, really, and redundant phrases.
Mark claims as Rule, Hypothesis, Example, Warning, Tested.
Mechanism: Prevents overclaiming and adds epistemic clarity.

Definition / What to try: Open with a concise question, answer one beat later.
Mechanism: Uses prediction and curiosity without clickbait.
Expected Direction: Higher retention if payoff is fast.

Definition / What to try: Partially bold only key concept words after compression.
Mechanism: Tests whether partial-word anchors add anything after compression.
Expected Direction: Small boost to recall or subjective clarity.
Failure Mode: May add noise; bionic evidence weak.

Definition / What to try: Bold full operator words: Not, Stop, First, Less.
Highlight only 5–15% of displayed words.
Mechanism: Reduces search cost without turning everything salient.

Definition / What to try: Use distinct emphasis for numbers, percentages, steps, dates.
Mechanism: Numbers create decision hooks and are easy to remember.
Expected Direction: Better exact recall for quantitative claims.

Definition / What to try: Break lines by meaning, not by width alone.
Normal weight for context, bold for decision word.

Reference Label: TYP-007 / AOD — Accent Only on Delta
Definition / What to try: Highlight only what changes from old belief to new belief.
Failure Mode: Requires accurate detection of old/new model.

Definition / What to try: Dull background words and keep key terms high opacity.
Place text on a stable capsule/plate over busy video.
Failure Mode: May obscure visual content or look heavy.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Plate vs no plate on busy backgrounds.

Definition / What to try: Use outline/shadow only when background contrast is unstable.
Failure Mode: Can look noisy if too thick.

Definition / What to try: Assign stable colors to roles: problem, cause, fix.
Failure Mode: Color alone is inaccessible; must pair with label/shape.

Definition / What to try: Use small shapes/icons for warning, rule, action, proof.
Failure Mode: Icons may distract or seem childish.

Definition / What to try: Compare clean sans, condensed bold, handwritten, meme style.
Mechanism: Typography may affect credibility and platform-native feel.
Expected Direction: Different winning fonts by content type.

Reference Label: TYP-014 / VWAR — Variable Weight Attention Ramp
Definition / What to try: Gradually increase weight on words as they become relevant.
Mechanism: Smooth cueing instead of sudden pop.

Definition / What to try: Use case intentionally: title case for labels, sentence case for ideas.
Failure Mode: All-caps may reduce readability and feel shouty.

Reference Label: TYP-016 / PAT — Punctuation as Timing Cue
Definition / What to try: Use periods, arrows, colons, and line breaks as rhythm markers.
Mechanism: Supports phrase parsing and temporal expectation.
Failure Mode: TikTok-native styles may omit punctuation, conflicting with clarity.

Definition / What to try: Pair a simple icon with a concept word: friction, trigger, proof.
Mechanism: Dual coding may aid memory if not decorative.

Definition / What to try: Test no bionic, one word bionic, all concepts bionic, all words bionic.
Expected Direction: Sparse bionic may win; heavy loses.
Failure Mode: If no effect, drop bionic component.

Definition / What to try: Avoid styling that exaggerates uncertain claims.
Auto-check caption/backplate contrast per frame.
Failure Mode: May force ugly backplates in complex frames.

Definition / What to try: Show compressed meaning 200–400 ms before the speaker lands it.
Mechanism: Reduces prediction load and primes interpretation.
Failure Mode: Too early feels spoiler-like or mismatched.

Definition / What to try: Show the overlay exactly when the semantic beat is spoken.
Failure Mode: May be slightly late for fast comprehension.

Definition / What to try: Display a summary immediately after the sentence.
Mechanism: Acts as recap rather than preview.
Failure Mode: Late text may miss retention window.

Definition / What to try: Place heavier text only during speech pauses or low-action moments.
Mechanism: Avoids competing with important audiovisual input.
Failure Mode: Pauses may be too rare in fast speech.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Same overlay during speech vs pauses.

Definition / What to try: Use natural speech breaths to segment caption events.
Mechanism: Matches human prosody and reduces rush.
Failure Mode: Breaths are hard to detect in noisy audio.

Definition / What to try: Reveal caption on visual cut or camera movement boundary.
Mechanism: Uses visual reset for attention capture.
Failure Mode: Can compete with scene change if too much text.

Definition / What to try: No caption shorter than 0.8 sec except one-word impact beats.
Insert brief no-text gaps between semantic beats.
Mechanism: Lets eyes return to video and reduces continuous reading.
Expected Direction: Better visual recall and lower fatigue.

Definition / What to try: Avoid presenting two critical words within 200–500 ms.
Expected Direction: Better recall of second critical word.

Definition / What to try: Decrease word count as speech tempo rises.
Failure Mode: May omit too much at high speed.

Definition / What to try: After an emphasized word, hold visual quiet for a beat.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Immediate next text vs refractory gap.

Definition / What to try: Hold final takeaway for the last 0.8–1.5 sec.
End with a phrase that reinterprets the opening hook.
Failure Mode: Can become manipulative if hook was weak.

Definition / What to try: Detect playback speed or speech rate and adjust overlay density.
Failure Mode: Platform may not expose playback speed.

Definition / What to try: Use no text during important sounds, laughs, or music drops.
Mechanism: Prevents text from flattening audio moments.
Failure Mode: Muted viewers lose context unless captions provide sound cue.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Text during sound vs no text + sound cue.

Definition / What to try: Never cover the mouth during speech-heavy clips.
Avoid covering eyes in emotional/storytelling clips.
Avoid hands, products, joints, cursor, and key action path.
Mechanism: Prevents text from stealing task-critical visual information.
Expected Direction: Better visual recall and task learning.

Definition / What to try: Place short labels next to the relevant object instead of bottom captions.
Define TikTok/Reels/Shorts danger zones and avoid them.
Place text away from predicted high-saliency regions unless it is a label.
Failure Mode: Low-saliency areas may be poor contrast.

Definition / What to try: Place text where it supports the semantic region of the scene.
Mechanism: Combines semantic value with gaze tendencies.
Expected Direction: Better comprehension than visually loud placement.

Definition / What to try: Avoid placing text near other text, UI, or high-frequency background.
Use empty background regions for semantic overlays.
Failure Mode: Not every shot has negative space.

Reference Label: GAZ-010 / SZTH — Shoulder-Zone Talking Head Overlay
Definition / What to try: Place captions near upper chest/shoulder, away from mouth/eyes/UI.
Mechanism: Balances face access and text proximity.
Expected Direction: Better than bottom captions for talking heads.
Failure Mode: May cover gestures or clothing patterns.

Definition / What to try: Place labels near the cursor/control being discussed.
Mechanism: Reduces split attention in UI tutorials.

Definition / What to try: Place short labels in a ring around product without covering it.
Mechanism: Keeps product visible while explaining attributes.

Reference Label: GAZ-013 / FAP — Foveal Anchor + Peripheral Cue
Definition / What to try: Put readable text centrally; use peripheral arrows/icons for orientation.
Expected Direction: Better orientation without peripheral reading burden.

Reference Label: GAZ-014 / DRAR — Dynamic Reflow Around ROI
Definition / What to try: Move captions frame-by-frame around faces/hands/products.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Static safe zone vs dynamic reflow.

Definition / What to try: Use different placement rules for talking head, demo, screen recording, story.
Move text near active speaker in multi-speaker clips.
Failure Mode: Motion may annoy frequent subtitle users.

Definition / What to try: Place overlay within triangle between speaker’s eyes, hands/object, and center.
Automatically reposition overlays for TikTok, Reels, Shorts, YouTube preview.
Detect overlap with native captions, stickers, captions, and UI.
After text appears, reserve moments where nothing blocks the face/object.
Mechanism: Lets gaze return to visual information.
Failure Mode: Muted viewers may need text continuity.

Definition / What to try: Animate the relation: problem enters, cause connects, fix resolves.
Move cause toward effect with arrow/line animation.
Visually flip from false belief to correction.
Make the word friction move slowly or resist movement.
Action word snaps into place when instruction lands.
Failure Mode: Can feel like generic social editing.

Definition / What to try: Reveal sequence vertically: cue → action → reward.
Use motion type to encode grammar: contrast slides apart, cause connects.
Keep key ideas static while less important transitions move.
Mechanism: Static becomes signal in a noisy platform.

Definition / What to try: Only one word pops per beat.
Replace active word-by-word highlighting with semantic beats for dense content.
Mechanism: Avoids forced tracking of every word.

Definition / What to try: Caption moves with object or hand only when labeling it.
Reveal instruction exactly when hand gesture demonstrates it.
Suppress text during major zoom/camera movement.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Text during zoom vs delayed text.

Definition / What to try: Briefly freeze/slow frame for a complex takeaway.
Mechanism: Gives time to encode dense idea.

Definition / What to try: Limit animated overlay events per 10 seconds.
Failure Mode: Too low may feel flat on platforms.

Reference Label: VLD-001 / VLAW — Visual-Load Adaptive Word Count
Definition / What to try: Use fewer overlay words when the frame is visually busy.
Expected Direction: Better comprehension in demos and complex scenes.

Definition / What to try: During demonstrations, show only verbs or labels: twist, lock, pull.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Sentence captions vs labels in demo.

Definition / What to try: Add a soft plate behind text only when background is complex.
Temporarily dim non-essential background behind text.
Compute frame-level complexity and use it to regulate overlay density.
Failure Mode: Complexity metric may not match human load.

Definition / What to try: Detect when too many text layers exist simultaneously.
Reduce caption animation and density during high visual motion.
Failure Mode: Could miss key speech during action.

Definition / What to try: Limit screen to one primary text block plus one micro-label.

Reference Label: VLD-009 / NCCC — Native Caption Conflict Checker
Definition / What to try: Avoid overlay collisions with platform auto-captions or imported captions.
Mechanism: Separates full caption layer and semantic layer.

Definition / What to try: Flag stripes, grids, leaves, and high-frequency textures behind text.
Mechanism: Crowding and contrast risk is high on patterned backgrounds.

Definition / What to try: Auto adjust text/backplate contrast per frame.
Limit total seconds of on-screen text per 10 seconds of video.
Mechanism: Prevents turning video into reading task.
Expected Direction: Better visual recall and lower fatigue.
Failure Mode: Muted viewers may need more text.

Reference Label: VLD-013 / DVG — Density by Viewer Goal
Definition / What to try: Use heavier overlays for learning clips, lighter for entertainment clips.

Reference Label: VLD-014 / CSEF — Caption Safe Empty Frames
Definition / What to try: Intentionally reserve clean frames for text instead of adding text anywhere.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Planned empty frames vs reactive overlays.

Definition / What to try: Annotate every point where a tester missed or struggled with text.
When audio is expected, overlay only cues and structure, not full language.
Expected Direction: Better visual recall with similar gist.

Definition / What to try: Overlay carries enough meaning without sound.
Failure Mode: May be too much text for audio-on viewers.

Definition / What to try: Full closed captions plus separate semantic overlay.
Mechanism: Preserves accessibility while enabling retention design.
Failure Mode: Can create clutter if poorly placed.

Definition / What to try: Show [laughter], [music rises], [door slam] only when meaningful.
Expected Direction: Better DHH access and story context.

Definition / What to try: Map speaker emphasis/intensity to controlled text weight or motion.
Use no overlay during silence or facial reaction, then land a short caption.
If background music/noise is high, increase caption completeness.
Mechanism: Compensates when speech is hard to hear.

Definition / What to try: Avoid text changes on every music beat unless text is music/lyric content.
Use small icons for sarcasm, warning, encouragement only when tone is ambiguous.
Mechanism: Adds affective context without long text.

Definition / What to try: For AR/wearables, use vibration patterns for emotion or emphasis.
Expected Direction: Better affective access in DHH contexts.
Failure Mode: Not relevant to normal TikTok yet.

Definition / What to try: Ask a 1-question recall prompt at the end: What reduces friction?
Mechanism: Retrieval strengthens memory and reveals comprehension.

Definition / What to try: Before solution, ask viewer to predict cause/fix.
Expected Direction: Better retention if answer arrives fast.
Failure Mode: May annoy viewers seeking quick answer.

Reference Label: MEM-003 / QCE — Quick Check End Card
Definition / What to try: End with micro quiz: Motivation or structure?

Reference Label: MEM-004 / SRAS — Spaced Recap Across Series
Definition / What to try: Repeat same memory phrase across multiple videos with variation.
Always use a small visual map: Problem → Cause → Fix.
Start with full labels, then fade to minimal cues as viewer learns.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Full labels vs fading labels over series.

Definition / What to try: Add one compressed analogy for abstract ideas.
Expected Direction: Better delayed recall for abstract concepts.

Definition / What to try: Show 2–4 nodes connected during complex explanations.
Show old state → new state visually/textually.
Pause briefly after an instruction before giving answer/example.
Failure Mode: May lower retention if too slow.

Reference Label: MEM-011 / SALS — Save Cue as Learning Signal
Definition / What to try: Use a save-worthy rule card, not a begging call-to-action.
Turn takeaways into memorable parallel structures or rhymes.
Convert advice into 2–3 checklist items at end.
Show two-axis quick diagnosis: effort high / clarity low.
Quickly contrast a wrong and right execution.
Always keep full captions available when using creative overlays.
Minimum Viable Experiment: User tests with overlay only vs dual layer.

Definition / What to try: Use simpler vocabulary and more context for second-language viewers.
Failure Mode: May feel too simple for natives.

Definition / What to try: Use domain shorthand for expert audiences.
Add labels for unfamiliar concepts before compressing heavily.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Labels vs no labels for beginners.

Definition / What to try: Compare bionic, LARF-like annotation, spacing, and plain compression for dyslexic viewers.
Mechanism: Different readability interventions may help specific groups.
Failure Mode: Risk of overclaiming without subgroup testing.

Definition / What to try: Provide a static/low-animation overlay for viewers sensitive to motion.

Reference Label: ACC-007 / OVD — Older Viewer Duration Mode
Definition / What to try: Increase caption duration and font size for older viewers.
Mechanism: Accounts for slower visual processing/reading needs.

Definition / What to try: Never rely on color alone; pair with labels/icons/shape.
Allow overlays to persist longer or simplify if viewer often pauses/rewatches.
Switch overlay mode based on audio-on/off, device, language, and content category.
LLM extracts proposition, role, and beat boundaries from transcript.
Failure Mode: LLM may hallucinate or distort meaning.

Definition / What to try: Auto-generates transcript, keyword, compression, BCC, BCC+, and controls.
Predict likely gaze heatmaps and avoid high-conflict zones.
Expected Direction: Better visual recall and fewer occlusions.
Failure Mode: Saliency may not equal semantic importance.

Definition / What to try: Detect visual regions that should not be covered.
Estimate per-frame visual complexity to choose word count.
Failure Mode: Metric may not match human load.

Reference Label: AIA-006 / GSWE — Gaze Simulation Without Eye Tracker
Definition / What to try: Use saliency/meaning maps to simulate likely gaze conflict.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Model score vs user gaze subset.

Definition / What to try: Generate and track variants across platforms.
Score compression, anchors, placement, load, contrast, muted clarity.
Failure Mode: Scores may reward rules over outcomes.

Definition / What to try: Reusable templates for TikTok/Reels/Shorts/Stories UI zones.
Suggest percent transcript to display based on speech rate/visual load.

Reference Label: AIA-011 / RWET — Remote Webcam Eye-Tracking Pilot
Definition / What to try: Use webcam gaze estimation for rough large-sample placement tests.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Webcam vs self-report vs subset eye tracker.

Definition / What to try: Combine recall results and gaze/text dwell to estimate efficiency.
Mechanism: Optimizes for cognitive value, not watch time alone.

Definition / What to try: Renders overlays based on detected shot type and visual load.
AI proposes; editor approves proposition, anchor, placement, timing.
For research/health/finance clips, overlay flags if claim is advice, evidence, or speculation.
Minimum Viable Experiment: No risk note vs risk-tagged version.

Definition / What to try: Use first 0.5–1.5 sec as a semantic promise.
Mechanism: Reduces early uncertainty and stops scroll.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Hook card vs no hook card.

Definition / What to try: Deliver the first useful idea within 2–3 seconds.

Reference Label: RET-003 / OLER — Open-Loop With Early Resolution
Definition / What to try: Open a gap, resolve quickly, then deepen.
Show Problem → Cause → Fix progress.
Mechanism: Reduces uncertainty about where video is going.

Definition / What to try: Tiny labels for 30–90s clips: Setup, Mistake, Fix, Rule.
Use final screen as a self-contained takeaway or checklist.
Ask a non-trivial answer prompt tied to video concept.
Test clean professional vs TikTok-native emoji/non-standard style.
At 40–60%, add a new semantic hook or map update.
End with text that connects to first frame without hiding final meaning.
Reject overlays that increase watch time by confusion instead of clarity.
Expected Direction: Better trust and long-term audience quality.

Definition / What to try: Use complete burned-in transcript as baseline.
Mechanism: Baseline for exact wording and accessibility-adjacent display.
Expected Direction: Best exact recall, worse visual efficiency.

Definition / What to try: Highlight important words inside full transcript.
Active word highlight following speech.
Expected Direction: May perform well for rhythm; worse for learning.

Definition / What to try: Apply bionic partial bolding to most words.
One word at a time in same spot.
Mechanism: Tests speed-reading style in video context.

Definition / What to try: Every word moves or pops.

Reference Label: CTL-007 / NNO — No Overlay / Native Captions Only
Definition / What to try: Baseline platform state.
Use color without labels/icons for semantic roles.
Expected Direction: Likely failure for color-blind/low contrast contexts.

Definition / What to try: Always lower-third regardless of content.
Make random words visually pop.
Mechanism: Tests whether salience without meaning helps.

Definition / What to try: Multiple overlays/captions/stickers simultaneously.
Use platform-native non-standard typography and emoji.
Expected Direction: Could increase engagement for informal categories.
Minimum Viable Experiment: Compare with clean captioning by category.
